fix: Profile upload improvements — error surfacing, HEIC support, diagnostics endpoint (#72)

* feat: US-001 - Surface server error messages in client upload function

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: US-002 - Add HEIC codec support to Alpine Docker image

Switch Dockerfile from node:24-alpine to node:24-trixie-slim (Debian 13)
and build libvips 8.17.3 from source with libheif + libde265 to enable
HEIC image decoding. The prebuilt sharp binaries only include the AVIF
codec (libaom); HEIC requires the libde265 codec which must be built
into libvips from source.

Changes:
- Build stage: install meson/ninja/dev-headers, compile libvips 8.17.3
  from source with HEIC support, then build sharp native addon against it
- Runtime stage: install system libheif + libde265 plugin + other runtime
  deps, copy custom-built libvips and its vips-heif module
- sharp.format.heif.input.fileSuffix now includes .heic, .heif, .avif
- JPEG/PNG/WebP support unchanged (no regression)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: US-003 - Create diagnostics route for storage and sharp health

Add GET /diagnostics endpoint (requireAuth-protected) that reports:
- sharp version, HEIF support status, and supported file suffixes
- Storage bucket name, existence check, and credentials presence
- Graceful error handling per section (no crash on partial failure)

[Repo Assist] Add tests for Series.scanValues, scanAllValues, foldValues, and reduceValues (#599)

🤖 *This PR was created by Repo Assist, an automated AI assistant.*

## Summary

Adds 7 new tests covering four public `Series` functions that previously
had zero test coverage: `scanValues`, `scanAllValues`, `foldValues`, and
`reduceValues`.

### Tests added

| Test | What it verifies |
|---|---|
| `Series.scanValues accumulates running sum correctly` | Basic
accumulation over a complete series |
| `Series.scanValues skips missing values and leaves them missing` |
Missing entries remain missing in the output; accumulator carries
forward from the last present value |
| `Series.foldValues computes running product correctly` | Basic
aggregate over a complete series |
| `Series.foldValues skips missing values` | Missing entries are
silently skipped |
| `Series.reduceValues computes sum of series` | Basic reduce over a
complete series |
| `Series.reduceValues skips missing values` | Missing entries are
silently skipped |
| `Series.scanAllValues sees missing values and can carry forward` |
`scanAllValues` exposes missing entries as `None` to the user function,
allowing custom carry-forward logic |

All tests use `Series.ofOptionalObservations` to create series with
structural missing values where needed.

commit.message:
fix: resolve workspace __placeholder__ routing and update specialist prompts

- Fix workspace and kanban page clients to extract real workspaceId from
  window.location.pathname when useParams() returns '__placeholder__' in
  static export mode (matches existing pattern in session-page-client.tsx)
- Add AGENTS.md co-author/commit discipline to crafter, developer, gate specialists
- Add missing 'name' frontmatter field to pr-analyzer.md
- Bump tauri.conf.json version from 0.2.3 to 0.2.4 (sync with package.json)

Root cause: Rust static server serves workspace/__placeholder__.html for all
/workspace/[id] URLs; Next.js useParams() returns '__placeholder__' during
initial hydration. The workspace-page-client then couldn't find the workspace
in the DB and redirected to '/'. Solution: read real ID from window.location.

commit.message:
Clarify AGENTS.md vs pull_request commitlint enforcement (#17)

Copilot PRs were repeatedly failing the `Conventional Commits`
pull_request check, and AGENTS.md did not make it clear why. This
updates the agent guidance to distinguish between what agents may read
for instructions and what CI actually enforces.

- **Problem clarified**
- Documents that `AGENTS.md` is guidance for coding agents, not a CI
policy file.
- States explicitly that the `pull_request` workflow enforces
Conventional Commits separately via `commitlint`.

- **Failure mode documented**
- Calls out that `.github/workflows/ci.yml` checks commit messages
across the PR range with `bunx commitlint --from ... --to ...`.
- Clarifies that the current workflow does **not** validate the PR
title.

- **Actionable guidance added**
- Adds a concrete Conventional Commit example for progress/manual
commits so agent-generated updates do not trip the PR check.

```md
- AGENTS.md is guidance for coding agents. CI does not read this file directly: on `pull_request`, `.github/workflows/ci.yml` runs `bunx commitlint --from ... --to ...` and fails if any commit message in the PR is not a Conventional Commit. The current workflow does **not** validate the PR title. When reporting progress or committing manually, always use Conventional Commit headers such as `docs: clarify AGENTS commitlint expectations`.
```

commit.message:
[Repo Assist] Fix GetRowsAs for C# interfaces; optimize Stats expanding/sparse-sums (#594)

🤖 *This PR was created by Repo Assist, an automated AI assistant.*

## Summary

Two independent improvements bundled into a single PR:

---

### Fix 1: `GetRowsAs` now works with C# interfaces (issue #503)

**Root cause:** `createTypedRowCreator` in `VectorHelpers.fs` called
`rowType.GetMethods()` and rejected any method that wasn't a
`get_`-prefixed property accessor. For C# types this caused spurious
failures:

- **C# classes/structs** have `GetHashCode`, `ToString`, `Equals`,
`GetType` — none are property getters → error.
- **C# interfaces with read-write properties** have both `get_X` and
`set_X` → `set_X` failed the check.

**Fix:**
1. Added an explicit `rowType.IsInterface` guard with a clear,
descriptive error message for non-interface types.
2. Filtered `rowType.GetMethods()` to `propGetterMethods` (only
`get_`-prefixed special methods) throughout the function. Only these
become data columns.
3. All remaining abstract interface methods (setters, methods from
parent interfaces) receive stub implementations that throw
`NotImplementedException`, satisfying the interface contract without
mapping them to columns.

**Example — now works:**
````csharp
public interface IStockRow { string Symbol { get; } double Price { get; set; } }
var rows = frame.GetRowsAs(IStockRow)(); // previously threw "Only readonly properties…"
```

---

### Fix 2: Performance improvements to `Stats.fs`

#### `initSumsSparse` — eliminated intermediate allocations

**Before:** `Array.choose OptionalValue.asOption |> Array.map toFloat |>
initSumsDense moment`
- Allocates 2 intermediate arrays (up to N elements each)
- Makes up to 6 passes over the data (choose + map + up to 4 passes in
initSumsDense)

**After:** Single `for` loop that accumulates all required sums in one
pass with no intermediate allocations.

Affects all aggregate stats on non-dense series: `Stats.mean`,
`Stats.variance`, `Stats.stdDev`, `Stats.skew`, `Stats.kurt`.

#### `expandingMin` / `expandingMax` — removed lazy Seq overhead

**Before:** `(Seq.scan fn nan) >> (Seq.skip 1) >> Array.ofSeq`
- Creates two intermediate lazy `seq` wrappers before materialising to
array

**After:** Direct `ResizeArray` accumulator loop — single pass, no lazy
infrastructure.

---

## Test Status

Code compiles and all manual verification tests pass on .NET 8.0:

```
Test 1: Read-only interface...                  PASSED
Test 2: Interface with setter...                PASSED
Test 3: Non-interface type should throw...      PASSED
Test 4: Stats.expandingMin...                   PASSED
Test 5: Stats.expandingMax...                   PASSED
Test 6: Stats.mean...                           PASSED
Test 7: Stats.stdDev...                         PASSED
````

The project's own test suite targets `net5.0` (the installed runtime in
CI is 8.0+), so `dotnet test` cannot be executed in this environment. CI
should run the full suite on the appropriate runtime.

Existing F# tests for `expandingMin`, `expandingMax` (in `Stats.fs`
tests), `GetRowsAs` (in `Frame.fs` tests), and `movingMin/Max` all cover
the changed code paths.

Closes #503

commit.message:
[Repo Assist] Fix Frame.indexRowsWith to produce missing values for extra row keys (#595)

🤖 *This PR was created by Repo Assist, an automated AI assistant.*

## Summary

Fixes `Frame.indexRowsWith` to correctly handle the case where the
caller provides more row keys than the frame currently has rows. Extra
keys now produce rows with missing values, consistent with user
expectations.

Closes #498

---

## Root Cause

`indexRowsWith` built a `VectorConstruction.GetRange` command that
always spanned the *original* row range. When the caller supplied a
longer key sequence the new `RowIndex` had more entries than the
resulting data vectors (length = original row count), producing an
internally inconsistent frame. Downstream operations like
`Frame.fillMissingWith` either silently no-opped or threw:

```
System.InvalidOperationException: Index and vector of a series should have the same length!
```

## Fix

When `newCount > existingCount`, the vector command is changed to:

```fsharp
VectorConstruction.Append(existing, VectorConstruction.Empty(newCount - existingCount))
```

This appends an all-missing segment so the vector length matches the new
row index. When `newCount <= existingCount` the original behaviour is
preserved.

An empty-frame edge-case (`existingCount = 0`) is handled with
`VectorConstruction.Empty newCount`.

## Trade-offs

- **No API change** — `indexRowsWith` accepts the same arguments as
before; only the output for longer key sequences changes from "broken
frame" to "frame with missing rows".
- `indexColsWith` already validates that key count must equal column
count; `indexRowsWith` now has richer semantics (pad with missing)
instead of strict validation, matching the reasonable user expectation
demonstrated in #498.

## Test Status

A regression test was added in `tests/Deedle.Tests/Frame.fs` that:
- verifies `RowCount` is the new key count
- checks that extra rows have missing values
- confirms `fillMissingWith 0.0` fills the extra rows correctly
- confirms original rows are unchanged

**Build:** ✅ `dotnet build src/Deedle/Deedle.fsproj -c Release` —
succeeded, 0 errors
**Test compile:** ✅ `dotnet build tests/Deedle.Tests/Deedle.Tests.fsproj
-c Release` — succeeded, 0 errors
**Test run:** ⚠️ Not runnable locally — the test project targets
`net5.0` but only .NET 8+ is available in this environment. CI (once PR
#590 upgrading to .NET 8 lands) will run the full test suite.

commit.message:
[Repo Assist] Fix Select not supported in mapFrameRowVector (issue #545) (#587)

🤖 *This is an automated PR from Repo Assist.*

Fixes the bug reported in #545 where `Series.mapValues` (and any other
transform using `Select`) on a series produced by `Frame.GetRowsAs<'T>`
throws:

```
System.Exception: mapFrameRowVector: Select not supported
```

## Root Cause

`Frame.GetRowsAs<'T>` returns a `Series<'K, 'T>` backed internally by a
`mapFrameRowVector` anonymous `IVector<'T>`. This vector wraps typed row
accessors generated via Reflection.Emit. The `Select` method on that
vector was simply not implemented — it always threw `failwith
"mapFrameRowVector: Select not supported"`.

Any series operation that calls `Select` internally (e.g.
`Series.mapValues`, `Series.mapKeys`, `|> Series.map`, etc.) would fail.

## Fix

Implement `Select` on `mapFrameRowVector` to return a new anonymous
`IVector<'TNew>` that:

- **`GetValue(a)`** — applies `g` at the given address
- **`GetValueAtLocation(l)`** — applies `g` using the full
`IVectorLocation`
- **`Data`** — lazily sequences all mapped values
- **`Select(g2)`** — chains back through the original
`mapFrameRowVector` data by composing `g` and `g2`: `self.Select(fun loc
v -> g2 loc (g loc v))`. This avoids deep wrapping of anonymous vectors.
- **`Invoke`** — not supported (consistent with the original)

## Trade-offs

- The chained `Select` composition is correct: each additional `Select`
call on the result adds one extra closure call per element, but avoids
allocating a new anonymous vector object per element.
- `GetValue(a)` constructs a `KnownLocation(a, int64 a)` as the location
passed to `g`. For standard linear addressing (the common case), address
and offset are the same value so this is exact. For non-linear schemes
the offset would be approximate, but `g` functions that ignore the
offset (e.g. `mapValues`) are unaffected.

## Tests Added

Two new tests in `tests/Deedle.Tests/Frame.fs`:
- `Can mapValues on a GetRowsAs series (issue #545)` — verifies
`Series.mapValues id` succeeds
- `Can transform a GetRowsAs series to a different type (issue #545)` —
verifies mapping to a derived value works

## Test Status

- ✅ `dotnet build src/Deedle/Deedle.fsproj` — succeeded (0 errors, 2
pre-existing warnings)
- ✅ `dotnet build tests/Deedle.Tests/Deedle.Tests.fsproj` — succeeded (0
errors)
- ⚠️ Test suite execution: test project targets `net5.0`, not available
in this environment (.NET 8+ installed). Tests will run in CI.

Closes #545

commit.message:
[Repo Assist] Fix OptionalValue(T) columns ignoring CultureInfo in Frame.SaveCsv (#583)

🤖 *This is an automated PR from Repo Assist.*

Fixes the bug reported in #544 where `OptionalValue(float)` (and similar
`OptionalValue(T)`) columns in a data frame are serialised without
honouring the `CultureInfo` passed to `Frame.SaveCsv`.

## Root Cause

`Frame.SaveCsv` uses `formatOptional` in `FrameUtils.writeCsv` to
convert each cell to a string. The function checks whether the column's
element type implements `IFormattable` and, if so, calls
`o.ToString(null, ci)` with the user-supplied culture.

For a plain `float` column this works correctly. But when a field is
typed as `OptionalValue(float)`, the column's `ElementType` is
`typeof(OptionalValue<float)>`. Since `OptionalValue<'T>` does **not**
implement `IFormattable`, the check fails and the code falls through to
`value.ToString()` — which calls `OptionalValue(float).ToString()` →
`float.ToString()` **without** a culture argument.

## Fix

A new `unwrapOptionalValue` helper detects `OptionalValue(T)` column
types (using `typedefof(OptionalValue<_)>`, the same pattern already
used elsewhere in the codebase), extracts the inner `HasValue`/`Value`
via reflection, and returns the inner type + inner value to
`formatOptional`. This lets the `IFormattable` branch format the
unwrapped value with the correct culture.

```fsharp
// Before — OptionalValue(float) ignored culture:
df.SaveCsv(path, separator = ';', culture = Globalization.CultureInfo("de-DE"))
// Output: Joe;"0,3372643363809795";0.3372643363809795   (float OK, OptionalValue(float) wrong)

// After:
// Output: Joe;"0,3372643363809795";"0,3372643363809795" (both correct)
```

The reflection call is per-row for `OptionalValue(T)` columns, which is
acceptable for CSV serialisation (I/O-bound). Columns that are not
`OptionalValue(T)` incur no overhead beyond a single `IsGenericType`
check.

## Test Status

- ✅ `dotnet build src/Deedle/Deedle.fsproj -c Release` — **succeeded**
(0 errors, 2 pre-existing warnings unchanged)
- ⚠️ Full test suite: the test project targets `net5.0`, which is not
available in this environment (only .NET 8+ installed). Tests will run
in CI. The fix was verified by code inspection.

Closes #544

commit.message:
[Repo Assist] Fix Frame.ofRecords for internal/private record types (#580)

🤖 *This is an automated PR from Repo Assist.*

Closes #562

## Root Cause

`Frame.ofRecords` (and related record-expansion functions) uses
reflection to discover properties and fields of record types. The search
was limited to `BindingFlags.Public`, but F# compiles property accessors
as **assembly-visible** (not strictly public) when the record type is
marked `internal`. As a result, `GetProperties(Public)` returned nothing
for `internal` records, silently producing an empty frame.

## Fix

In `src/Deedle/FrameUtils.fs`, `getExpandableProperties` and
`getExpandableFields` now include `BindingFlags.NonPublic` in the
reflection search, with targeted filters to avoid including unwanted
implementation-detail members:

- **Properties**: Excludes explicit interface implementations (property
names containing `.`, e.g.
`System.Runtime.CompilerServices.ITuple.Length`) and private getters.
Allows public or assembly-visible (`internal`) getters.
- **Fields**: Excludes compiler-generated backing fields (those carrying
`CompilerGeneratedAttribute`, e.g. F# record backing fields like `Day@`)
and private fields.
- `p.GetGetMethod(nonPublic=true)` is used to correctly retrieve
non-public property getter methods.

## Test Status

- ✅ `src/Deedle/Deedle.fsproj` builds successfully (0 errors)
- ✅ `tests/Deedle.Tests/Deedle.Tests.fsproj` builds successfully (0
errors)
- ✅ New test `Can read sequence of internal records` added and verified
manually
- ✅ Existing regression tests verified (public records and tuples still
produce correct columns)
- ⚠️ `.NET 5.0` is required to run the test suite via `dotnet fake
build`, but the CI environment has only .NET 8/9/10. This is a

defined in the same or another assembly. There is no change in behaviour

commit.message:
[Repo Assist] Fix Frame.GetRowKeyAt to take int instead of int64 (issue #523) (#588)

🤖 *This is an automated PR from Repo Assist.*

Fixes the API inconsistency reported in #523.

## Problem

`Frame.GetRowKeyAt(index)` inferred `index` as `int64` because
`IIndex.AddressAt` takes `int64`. However:

- The XML documentation says *"Offset (**integer**) of the row key to be
returned"* — i.e. `int`
- `Frame.GetRowAt<'T>(index)` uses an explicit `int` parameter with
`int64 index` cast
- `Series.GetKeyAt(index)` uses `int`

This means anyone calling `frame.GetRowKeyAt(someIntVariable)` would get
a compile error unless they added an explicit `int64` cast, which is
surprising and inconsistent.

## Fix

Add an explicit `int` type annotation to the parameter and a `int64
index` cast when calling `AddressAt`, matching the existing pattern in
`GetRowAt<'T>`:

```fsharp
// Before (index inferred as int64)
member frame.GetRowKeyAt(index) =
  frame.RowIndex.KeyAt(frame.RowIndex.AddressAt index)

// After (index explicitly int, consistent with Series.GetKeyAt and Frame.GetRowAt)
member frame.GetRowKeyAt(index: int) =
  frame.RowIndex.KeyAt(frame.RowIndex.AddressAt(int64 index))
```

## Breaking Change

⚠️ **Minor breaking change**: callers that explicitly pass `int64`
values (e.g. `frame.GetRowKeyAt(0L)` or
`frame.GetRowKeyAt(someInt64Var)`) will need to add an explicit cast:
`frame.GetRowKeyAt(int someInt64Var)`. Callers passing plain `int`
values (or integer literals like `42`) will now work without any cast.

## Test Status

- ✅ `dotnet build src/Deedle/Deedle.fsproj` — succeeded (0 errors, 2
pre-existing warnings)
- ✅ `dotnet build tests/Deedle.Tests/Deedle.Tests.fsproj` — succeeded (0
errors, no callers broken)
- ⚠️ Test suite execution: test project targets `net5.0`, not available
in this environment. Tests will run in CI.

Closes #523

commit.message:
[Repo Assist] Extract fsdocs-package-version dynamically from Directory.Build.props (#600)

🤖 *This PR was created by Repo Assist, an automated AI assistant.*

## Summary

Both `build.sh` and `build.ps1` previously hardcoded the documentation
package version as `fsdocs-package-version 4.0.0`. This creates a risk
of **version drift**: whenever `Directory.Build.props` is updated to
bump the library version, the build scripts also need a manual update —
and it's easy to forget.

## Change

Read the version from `Directory.Build.props` at build time:

- **`build.sh`**: uses `grep -oP` to extract the `(Version)` element
value
- **`build.ps1`**: uses PowerShell's `[xml]` cast to parse the props
file

This is the same source of truth used by the NuGet package build
(`dotnet pack`), so the documentation will always match the published
package version.

## Test Status

> **Note**: CI may fail on the `dotnet fsdocs build` step due to a
pre-existing XML syntax issue in doc comments (tracked in PR #597). This
failure is **not caused by this change** — it affects all open branches
equally. Once PR #597 is merged, CI should pass here too.

The build and test steps (compile + unit tests) are unaffected by this
change.

commit.message:
[Repo Assist] Fix filterRows/filterRowValues/Where losing ColumnTypes when column is all-missing (issue #516) (#601)

🤖 *This PR was created by Repo Assist, an automated AI assistant.*

Fixes the bug reported in #516 where `frame.ColumnTypes` incorrectly
changes to `System.Object` after filtering rows when the resulting
column contains only missing values.

## Root Cause

`Frame.filterRows`, `Frame.filterRowValues`, and `Frame.Where` all
previously reconstructed the frame **from rows** (via
`FrameUtils.fromRowsAndColumnKeys`). That function uses a "witness
value" to determine the column type — it picks any non-missing value
from the column to drive type dispatch:

```fsharp
let someValue =
  nested |> Series.observations |> Seq.tryPick (fun (_, v) ->
    v.TryGetObject(key) |> OptionalValue.asOption)
let someValue = defaultArg someValue (obj())  // ← falls back to obj() when all missing
columnCreator key someValue
```

When **all values in a column are missing** after filtering,
`Seq.tryPick` returns `None`, the fallback becomes `obj()`, and the
resulting column vector has `ElementType = typeof(obj)`. Hence
`frame.ColumnTypes` reports `System.Object` instead of the original
type.

## Fix

Replace the row-reconstruction path in `filterRows`, `filterRowValues`,
and both `Where` overloads with the same **column-wise** approach
already used by `filterRowsBy` and `realignRows`:

1. Apply the predicate to collect the filtered row index.
2. Use `IndexBuilder.Reindex` to produce a `Relocate` vector command
that maps the new row addresses back to the original ones.
3. Apply `VectorHelpers.transformColumn` to each column vector — this
preserves the generic type parameter `'T` of each column regardless of
how many values are missing.

Also adds `open Deedle.Vectors` to `FrameExtensions.fs` so
`VectorConstruction.Return` is in scope.

## Behaviour preserved

- All values (present or missing) are correctly mapped to their new
positions.
- Empty filter result still yields an empty frame with the same column
keys and types.
- No change in behaviour for columns where at least one value is
non-missing.

## Test

New regression test `Filter rows preserves column types when all column
values are missing` in `tests/Deedle.Tests/Frame.fs` verifies that
column `ElementType` remains `float` after filtering to rows where a
column contains only `NaN` (missing) values.

## Test Status

- ✅ `dotnet build src/Deedle/Deedle.fsproj -c Release` — 0 errors
- ✅ `dotnet test tests/Deedle.Tests/Deedle.Tests.fsproj -c Release` —
**465 / 465 passed**

> ⚠️ CI docs-generation (`fsdocs`) will fail on this PR because the XML
doc issue in master (fixed by PR #597) has not yet been merged. This is
an infrastructure failure unrelated to this change — all tests pass.

Closes #516

[Repo Assist] Fix .editorconfig F# glob pattern (remove spaces in brace expansion) (#602)

🤖 *This PR was created by Repo Assist, an automated AI assistant.*

## Problem

The `.editorconfig` file had the following F# section:

```ini
[*.{fs, fsx, fsi}]
indent_style = space
indent_size = 2
```

Per the [EditorConfig spec]((editorconfig.org/redacted), glob patterns
inside `{}` are comma-separated **without spaces**. The pattern `[*.{fs,
fsx, fsi}]` (with spaces after commas) would match files with
space-prefixed extensions like `". fsx"` and `". fsi"`, **not** `.fsx`
or `.fsi`.

This means editors that strictly follow the spec (e.g. VS Code with the
EditorConfig plugin, IntelliJ, Rider) would not apply the `indent_size =
2` setting to `.fsx` and `.fsi` files — only `.fs` files would be
matched correctly.

## Fix

Remove the spaces:

```ini
[*.{fs,fsx,fsi}]
```

This is a one-character change per comma (two commas → two spaces
removed), matching the format already used in the other sections
(`[*.{csproj,proj,projitems,...}]` and `[*.{config,nuspec,resx}]`).

## Test Status

No build or test changes — this is a pure editor configuration file fix.
CI is unaffected.

[Repo Assist] Fix CSV schema parsing for column names containing parentheses (closes #348) (#604)

🤖 *This is an automated PR from Repo Assist.*

Fixes the regex bug in CSV schema parsing reported in #348.

## Root Cause

`nameAndTypeRegex` in `CsvInference.fs` was incorrectly using
`RegexOptions.RightToLeft`. This caused the regex engine to scan
right-to-left and match the **first** `(` it found (scanning from the
right), rather than the **last** `(` from the left.

For a schema item like `"Revenue (USD)(float)"`, the intended parse is:
- name = `"Revenue (USD)"`
- type = `"float"`

But with `RightToLeft`, the regex matched:
- name = `"Revenue "` (stopped at the inner `(` in `(USD)`)
- type = `"USD)(float"` (incorrect!)

Without `RightToLeft`, the standard greedy `.+` in the `name` group
correctly finds the **last** parenthesized group as the type, because it
consumes as many characters as possible before backtracking.

## Fix

Remove `RegexOptions.RightToLeft` from `nameAndTypeRegex` only (line
44). The other two regexes (`typeAndUnitRegex` and
`overrideByNameRegex`) correctly retain `RightToLeft` because they need
to handle cases like:
- `"float(unit<sub)>"` — unit string can contain `<`
- `"Revenue->newName=float"` — column names can contain `=`

## Test Status

All 465 existing tests pass ✅. A new regression test is added:

```fsharp
[(Test)]
let ``Can read CSV with schema where column name contains parentheses``() =
  let csv = "Revenue (USD),Count\n100.0,1\n200.0,2"
  use reader = new System.IO.StringReader(csv)
  let df = Frame.ReadCsv(reader, schema="Revenue (USD)(float),Count(int)")
  List.ofSeq df.ColumnKeys |> shouldEqual ["Revenue (USD)"; "Count"]
  df.GetColumn(float)("Revenue (USD)") |> Series.values |> List.ofSeq |> shouldEqual [100.0; 200.0]
```

## Trade-offs

None. The greedy (non-RightToLeft) behavior is the correct and intended
behavior for `nameAndTypeRegex`. This is a pure bug fix with no
behaviour changes for existing valid schemas.

This PR adds missing tests for two areas that had no coverage:

for this function at all. The new tests verify:
- The structural contract: `melt` produces a frame with `Row`, `Column`,

in the reconstructed frame

regression/documentation test addressing [issue

the existing `separators=" "` + `hasHeaders=false` API already handles
the reported use case correctly.

[Repo Assist] Fix Frame.ofRecords creating duplicate columns for F# records (#578)

🤖 *This is an automated PR from Repo Assist.*

Fixes the duplicate-column bug reported in #569.

## Root Cause

When the F# compiler lowers a record type to IL, it generates **both**:
- A public property `Open` (for user access)
- A compiler-generated backing field `Open@` (for internal storage,
decorated with `[(CompilerGenerated)]`)

`getExpandableFields` in `FrameUtils.fs` called `Type.GetFields` for all
public instance fields but did not filter out these compiler-generated
backing fields. As a result, `Frame.ofRecords` on an F# record emitted
each field **twice**: once from `getExpandableProperties` (the property)
and once from `getExpandableFields` (the compiler-generated field with
the `@`-suffixed name).

## Fix

One-line change in `getExpandableFields`: filter out any field decorated
with `System.Runtime.CompilerServices.CompilerGeneratedAttribute`:

```fsharp
let getExpandableFields (ty:Type) =
    ty.GetFields(BindingFlags.Instance ||| BindingFlags.Public)
    |> Array.filter (fun f ->
        f.GetCustomAttributes(typeof(System.Runtime.CompilerServices.CompilerGeneratedAttribute), false).Length = 0)
```

This leaves all genuine user-defined public fields (e.g. C#
auto-property backing fields) untouched while correctly excluding the
compiler-generated ones for F# records.

## Test

A new regression test `Frame.ofRecords does not create duplicate columns
for F# records` verifies that `Frame.ofRecords` on the existing `Price`
record type produces exactly 4 columns (one per field), with no
`@`-suffixed duplicates.

## Test Status

- ✅ `dotnet build src/Deedle/Deedle.fsproj` — succeeded (0 errors)
- ✅ `dotnet build tests/Deedle.Tests/Deedle.Tests.fsproj` — succeeded (0
errors)
- ⚠️ Test suite execution: the test project targets `net5.0`, which is
not available in this environment (only .NET 8+ installed). Tests will
run in CI. The fix logic was verified by inspection.

Closes #569
